channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Brewers owner poses shocking question about what his job is
item.description: Milwaukee Brewers owner Mark Attanasio posed a shocking question about what his job is as owner of the team before spring training games began.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASCAR deals Daytona 500 pole winner massive fine for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: Daytona 500 pole-winner Chase Briscoe was dealt a major penalty Wednesday when NASCAR said Joe Gibbs Racing had a modified spoiler on the No. 19 Toyota in time trials.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bets pour in on 4 Nations Cup final between US and Canada
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The players have been emotionally invested in the 4 Nations Face-Off, and sports bettors have noticed and followed suit.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 49ers star Deebo Samuel to host football camp at Bakersfield High School in June
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- San Francisco 49ers wide receiver Deebo Samuel is scheduled to teach the youth of Bakersfield at a football camp. Deebo Samuel's Youth Football Camp plans on coming to town June 13 to give kids hands-on football instruction and teach them more about football.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Eyraud, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin bans trans athletes from girls' sports, following Trump's executive order
item.description: Wisconsin's high school athletics association announced Wednesday it is banning trans athletes from girls' sports in order to comply with Trump's recent order.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With the Dodgers' organization watching, Roki Sasaki has some success throwing live batting practice
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Even in practice, Japanese phenom Roki Sasaki draws a crowd. The 23-year-old threw 27 pitches over two innings of live batting practice on Wednesday in front of hundreds of fans and several teammates.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: NFL projects salary cap between $277.5M and $281.5M in 2025, rising by as much as $26M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NFL salary cap will be between $277.5 million and $281.5 million for the 2025 season, rising as much as $26 million from last year. The bump is great news for Cincinnati, which is hoping to sign receiver Tee Higgins to a long-term contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luka Doncic is likely off his minutes restriction as the Lakers return to work against Charlotte
item.description: Luka Doncic is going back to work with the Los Angeles Lakers, and he’s likely back to full speed as well.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Raducanu says 'I'll be OK' after man removed from stands at Dubai tournament
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Emma Raducanu said she will be OK after what she called a “difficult experience” related to the presence of a man who had exhibited “fixated behavior” toward her at the Dubai Championships. The 2021 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justin Herbert, Chargers will play their 2025 season opener in Brazil
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Chargers will play their 2025 season opener in Brazil, making the franchise the first to play on five continents, the NFL announced Wednesday. The Chargers' opponent is expected to be announced in May, when the NFL's regular-season schedule is released.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: See: Rare 1970s photos from the birth of modern skateboarding in East Bay
item.description: A former East Bay high-school student shares his personal photos in "Montevideo: Skateboarding History in the San Ramon Valley.
item.dc:creator: John Metcalfe, The Mercury News

item.title: Mbappé hat trick for Real Madrid dumps Man City out of Champions League. PSG and Dortmund advance
item.description: Kylian Mbappé scored a hat trick, Erling Haaland stayed on the bench injured and Real Madrid has dumped Manchester City out of the Champions League.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Connecticut Sun don't plan on trading Marina Mabrey, who asked to be dealt this month
item.description: The Connecticut Sun don't plan on trading guard Marina Mabrey after she asked to be dealt earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohtani throws a 2nd bullpen session as he makes his way back to the mound for the Dodgers
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Shohei Ohtani threw his second bullpen session of spring training on Tuesday, continuing the process of his return to the mound for the Los Angeles Dodgers. Ohtani had his first bullpen session on Saturday. He hopes to pitch this season for the first time since Aug.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: 1st-year White Sox manager Will Venable goes 1-on-1 with GN Sports from spring training
item.description: PHOENIX — As first-year White Sox manager Will Venable begins to introduce himself to Chicago baseball fans, you can probably already state this about him with certainty: He'd beat any other MLB manager on the basketball court.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Right-hander Nick Pivetta, Padres finalize $55M four-year contract
item.description: Right-hander Nick Pivetta and the San Diego Padres officially announced a finalized $55 million, four-year contract on Monday, a backloaded deal that allows him to opt out after the 2026 and '27 seasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stephen Curry captures MVP honors in All-Star Game at home, leads Shaq’s OGs to victory
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The final pregame introduction belonged to none other than Stephen Curry. The big MVP trophy at the end, Curry heaved that high in the air, too, in a fitting finish to his special All-Star Game at home. His arena. His fans.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The winning total last year: 211 points. The winning total this year: 41 points. An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Padres baseball season in San Diego kicks off March 27 with opening series, East Village Block Party
item.description: The Padres are gearing up for the start of baseball season in San Diego and fans can get excited with the free East Village Block Party that takes place during opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Aggies lose heartbreaker at first place New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE (ABC4 Sports) - With first place in the Mountain West men’s basketball standings on the line, Utah State (22-4, 12-3 MW) fell just short on Sunday in Albuquerque, dropping an 82-79 thriller at The Pit. This marked the second time in two weeks the Aggies have lost to the Lobos.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to attend NASCAR's Daytona 500 for the second time as president
item.description: Donald Trump is planning to attend Sunday's Daytona 500 for the second time as president and help further burnish his image as a sports fan.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Runnin' Utes stun #17 Kansas, 74-67
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 24 points and became Utah's career leader in made 3-pointers as the Utes beat No. 17 Kansas 74-67 on Saturday night. Madsen's fifth and final 3-pointer of the game with 1:58 to play gave him the Utah career record of 288 made 3s.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Former White Sox All-Star, World Series Champion Bobby Jenks undergoing stomach cancer treatment
item.description: Bobby Jenks, a two-time All-Star closer and World Series champion with the Chicago White Sox, is being treated for stomach cancer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: Chula Vista 58, Sweetwater 56
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys basketball Chula Vista at Sweetwater with the Spartans edging out the Red Devils 58-56. Both teams now 4-1 in league play with the season winding down and league title still up for grabs.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NASCAR disqualifies Kligerman for too-low truck after flag-planting win at Daytona. He is appealing
item.description: Full-time television analyst and part-time NASCAR driver Parker Kligerman claimed the checkered flag for the Truck Series season opener and promptly planted it in the grass by the Daytona International Speedway logo.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Avid volunteer enjoys first Genesis Invitational
item.description: Jenny Liu has volunteered at Torrey Pines for 24 years. She’s seen a lot in her time, but this week it was her first experience with the Genesis Invitational.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Central girls & Grand Junction boys secure wins in rivalry showdown
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — In a thrilling matchup that went to overtime, the Central Warriors edged out Grand Junction 55-51, securing their sixth win of the season. Tied at 44 heading into the extra period, Bianca Berens opened with a mid-range jumper to give Central the lead.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Philadelphia Eagles Super Bowl parade
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On this Feb. 14, love Hurts. At least in Philadelphia, where swooning Eagles fans are ready to celebrate the Super Bowl champions and MVP quarterback Jalen Hurts across the City of Brotherly Love.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz fall to Clippers in final game before All-Star break
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Norman Powell scored a season-high 41 points and James Harden added 32 to lift the Los Angeles Clippers to a 120-116 overtime victory over the Utah Jazz on Thursday night. Harden also had 10 rebounds, seven assists and three steals.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Warriors kick off 2025 season next week
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - About to begin its eighth season in Major League Rugby, the Utah Warriors are out to improve upon last year's 5-11 record. "I think we do really well this year," said fullback Sione Mahe.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox sign veteran CF Michael A. Taylor to 1-year deal
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Center fielder Michael A. Taylor and the Chicago White Sox have agreed to a $1.95 million, one-year contract. Chicago announced the agreement Wednesday. Taylor spent last season with the Pittsburgh Pirates, batting .193 with five home runs, 21 RBI and a .543 OPS in 113 games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Meet Colorado's top scorer: Olathe junior Isaiah Gordon
item.description: No player in the entire state of Colorado scores more points per game than Olathe junior Isaiah Gordon.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Nick Pivetta agrees to $55 million, 4-year contract with San Diego Padres, AP source says
item.description: Nick Pivetta became the last of the high-profile free agent pitchers to reach an agreement this offseason, a backloaded $55 million, four-year contract with the San Diego Padres, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5
